title: About the Institute
subtitle: A public-interest institution for Active Inference education, research, and applications.
audience: Visitors who want a fast institutional orientation.
lede: The Active Inference Institute is a volunteer-led 501(c)(3) open science nonprofit making Active Inference known, taught, and applied across science, engineering, and practical domains.

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.body: AII supports education, research, training, and applications related to Active Inference. The Institute welcomes different backgrounds, time zones, and familiarity levels, and organizes the ecosystem through programs, projects, public media, research resources, and open-source collaboration.

sections.heading: Mission, Vision, Values, and Principles
sections.body: The Institute emphasizes integrity, open learning, responsible conduct, professional development, research excellence, and practical service. The public site presents these as an operating stance: make Active Inference more accessible, rigorous, and applicable while supporting ethical collaboration.

sections.heading: Public Structure
sections.body: The public Institute surface is organized around learning groups, project groups, media channels, public repositories, official shortlinks, and open collaboration pathways. This site emphasizes the external routes visitors can inspect and use directly.

sections.heading: Information Surfaces
sections.body: Public channels include Discord, official shortlinks, newsletters, video and podcast channels, social profiles, project repositories, and GitHub project pages. This website organizes those surfaces into stable visitor pathways.

cards.title: Open participation
cards.text: The Institute is designed for people who arrive with different time zones, skills, and familiarity levels.

cards.title: Public knowledge
cards.text: Livestreams, podcasts, readings, projects, and educational groups create reusable learning material.

cards.title: Applied rigor
cards.text: The Institute connects theoretical foundations with applied projects, tools, symposia, and ecosystem support.
